step_by_step_thinking: I concur that option A "Brittle fracture" accurately describes the mechanism leading up to tooth structure cutting under tensile forces. The key point here is how the accumulation of tensile stresses creates localized regions prone to cracking, followed by rapid expansion once those regions exceed their strength limits—characteristic features of brittle fracture processes. Additionally, it should be noted that unlike ductile materials which deform plastically prior to ultimate rupture, brittle materials fail suddenly after accumulating enough internal strain energy; this matches well with observations made regarding dental tissue responses to mechanical loads.
